text.text: Brenda B. reposted this

One of the biggest misconceptions about GLP-1 medications is that they automatically cause muscle loss. 
The truth? Muscle loss is preventable when weight loss is properly supported with:
 
✅ adequate protein 
✅ resistance training 
✅ supplementation 
✅ ongoing medical oversight 

In this InBody® scan example, one of our JumpstartMD members reduced body fat from 51 lbs → 32.1 lbs in 8 months while losing less than 1 lb of muscle mass. 

That’s the power of structured, medically supervised care. 

Interested in a clinician-guided approach to fat loss and body recomposition? Schedule a complimentary consultation: https://jsmd.us/4rG2IeU 

Proud to partner with InBody® (InBody USA) and grateful to have Lia Ha, PA-C, helping educate our community on how to lose fat without sacrificing strength. 

#GLP1 #BodyComposition #InBody #WeightLossMedicine #HormoneHealth #MetabolicHealth #JumpstartMD #ObesityMedicine #StrengthTraining #FatLoss

text.text: Brenda B. reposted this

Testosterone is often overlooked in conversations about perimenopause and menopause—but its impact on women’s health is significant 📊 As estrogen and progesterone decline, testosterone levels frequently drop as well, contributing to fatigue, diminished motivation, brain fog, loss of muscle mass, increased abdominal fat, and changes in sexual health. 

Despite the evidence, testosterone is rarely discussed or treated in women. At JumpstartMD, we take a comprehensive, data-driven approach to hormone care, ensuring women receive personalized treatment that supports both current quality of life and long-term health. 

👉 Visit https://jsmd.us/3Zfb7sO to learn more or schedule your free consultation today. 

#WomensHealth #MenopauseCare #Perimenopause #HormoneOptimization #TestosteroneForWomen #HealthcareInnovation #JumpstartMD

text.text: Brenda B. reposted this

Personalized weight loss starts with data — not guesswork. 

At JumpstartMD, our care model begins with a one-on-one session with a clinician to gather critical information, including labs, EKG, and body composition analysis. From there, clinicians and health coaches review each member’s medical history and results to design a truly individualized treatment plan. 

“As Chief Medical Officer and Founder, I believe sustainable health outcomes come from understanding the whole person — not applying one-size-fits-all solutions -- which is why we have members take comprehensive labs, a body composition analysis with our InBody scales, and review medical history to design a truly individualized treatment plan. Your body is unique, your weight care should be too” 

— Sean Bourke, MD
Chief Medical Officer & Founder, JumpstartMD

🚨 California HR Alert: 2026 Employment Laws Are Live

California employers — the compliance bar keeps rising in 2026. Here’s what HR teams should have on their radar 👇

🔹 Expanded paid leave & employee protections
💰 New wage & pay transparency requirements
🛑 Stronger anti-discrimination & retaliation rules
📋 Increased enforcement on worker classification
📌 Updated workplace notices & postings
🤖 Growing rules around workplace tech & AI use

✅ HR To-Do for 2026:
Audit policies • Review pay practices • Refresh manager training • Partner with legal counsel

Not legal advice.

#CaliforniaHR #HRCompliance #EmploymentLaw #PeopleOps #HRLeadership #2026Ready

text.text:
🚨 2026 Healthcare Trend Every CA HR Leader Should Watch

One of the biggest challenges heading into 2026 is the growing clinician shortage and rising burnout. Studies state that nearly 55% of U.S. healthcare workers are considering leaving their jobs due to stress and workload strain. 

Supporting our clinical teams needs to be at the core of our mission in order to support delivering compassionate, high‑quality care. 

As demand continues to rise across California, HR teams will need to focus on:

🕒 Flexible scheduling to support work‑life balance.
💛 Employee well‑being, including mental, physical, and financial support.
📈 Skills‑focused hiring & development, especially as telehealth and modern care models evolve. 

Strong teams = stronger patient outcomes.

Let’s make 2026 a year of supporting, empowering, and uplifting our healthcare workforce. 💙

#HealthcareHR #CaliforniaHealthcare #HRLeadership  #2026Trends

text.text: Brenda B. reposted this

🚨 California Healthcare Wage Updates – Effective January 1, 2026 🚨

California healthcare employers and employees should be aware of several overlapping minimum wage requirements that take effect or continue into 2026. Here’s a clear snapshot of what matters most ⬇️

🔹 California Healthcare Minimum Wage (SB 525)
Applies to covered healthcare facilities and workers
• $23.00–$25.00/hour, depending on facility type
• Many covered employers reach $25.00/hour by June 1, 2026
• Future annual increases are scheduled through 2033

🔹 California State Minimum Wage
• $17.00/hour (applies to all employers, regardless of size)

🔹 Salary Exempt Minimum (Executive, Administrative, Professional)
• Must equal 2× the state minimum wage
• $70,304 annually


🔹 Physician & Surgeon Minimum Wage (Exempt Status)
• Licensed physicians and surgeons must earn at least
$104.50 per hour
to qualify as exempt under California 

🔹 Local Healthcare & City Wage Ordinances
Local ordinances override state and SB 525 rates when higher
Common examples include:
• San Francisco
• Los Angeles
• Berkeley
• Emeryville
• San Jose

📌 Key takeaway: In California healthcare, employers must always pay the highest applicable wage — state, healthcare-specific, or local.

Staying compliant requires regular wage audits and close attention to facility classification and employee roles.

#CaliforniaHealthcare #MinimumWage2026 #SB525 #HealthcareCompliance #HR #EmploymentLaw

text.text:
🤰💼California Pregnancy Disability Leave (PDL): compliance is NOT optional. Brand new employees are eligible

A common compliance mistake I still see: assuming an employee isn’t eligible for Pregnancy Disability Leave because they’re “too new.”

In California, Pregnancy Disability Leave (PDL) applies from day one.

Even if an employee has only been with the company one month, employers may still be required to:
	•	Provide up to 4 months of job-protected PDL if medically necessary
	•	Engage in the interactive process
	•	Provide reasonable accommodations
	•	Maintain health benefits during PDL
	•	Protect against discrimination or retaliation

PDL is separate from FMLA and CFRA and does not have hours-worked or length-of-service requirements.

HR owns this compliance.
That means training managers, correcting assumptions, and responding quickly when pregnancy-related limitations are disclosed.

Getting PDL wrong isn’t just a policy issue — it’s a legal risk.

#CaliforniaHR #PregnancyDisabilityLeave #PDL #HRCompliance #EmploymentLaw

Excited for what we’re building. 🚀

text.text:
✨ California HR Check‑In: January 2026 Highlights + February Must‑Dos ✨

January came in hot for CA employers. A quick pulse check 👇

📌 What Changed as of Jan 1, 2026 (So Far)
✅ CalWARN notices expanded 
✅ Exempt salary minimum = $70,304/year (2× minimum wage)
✅ “Stay‑or‑Pay” agreements largely banned – training, sign‑on, & relocation paybacks need a hard review
✅ Expanded paid sick leave uses for victims of qualifying crimes & court proceedings
✅ Stronger enforcement on tip theft & unpaid wage judgments


🗓️ February 2026 HR Action Items
📄 Distribute the new “Workplace Know Your Rights” notice to all employees (and new hires)
💰 Audit payroll + exempt classifications for new wage thresholds
📑 Review offer letters, bonus plans & reimbursement agreements for AB 692 compliance
📘 Update handbooks & sick leave policies
🏙️ Confirm local minimum wages 


🎯 Bottom line: 2026 started with big compliance shifts. February is about auditing, updating, and documenting before enforcement catches up.

💬 What’s already on your HR to‑do list this month?

#CaliforniaHR #HRCompliance #EmploymentLaw #HumanResources #HRLeadership #2026Updates

text.text: Brenda B. reposted this

The updated U.S. Dietary Guidelines reflect a growing consensus in metabolic health and clinical nutrition: prioritize real, minimally processed foods, reduce added sugar and refined carbohydrates, increase protein intake, and allow flexibility for lower-carbohydrate approaches in metabolic disease. 

At JumpstartMD, this evidence-based framework has guided patient care for years — informed by clinical outcomes, metabolic science, and clinician-led protocols rather than diet trends. 

As nutrition science evolves, responsible medical care evolves with it. 

👉 Read more about our evidence-based approach to metabolic health: https://jsmd.us/4syBXtx

#MetabolicHealth #ClinicalNutrition #EvidenceBasedMedicine #WeightLossMedicine #JumpstartMD

text.text: Brenda B. reposted this

Why do New Year diets fail? According to Sean Bourke, MD, Founder and Chief Medical Officer of JumpstartMD, the problem isn’t discipline — it’s design. 

“The word diet comes from the Greek diaita, meaning a way of life — not a set of food rules.” 

Modern diets fail because biology isn’t one-size-fits-all. Differences in metabolism, hormones, sleep, stress, and activity determine how people respond to food — making short-term, universal plans unsustainable. 

“When we force everyone into the same plan, failure is predictable.” 

What works instead is a personalized, science-based approach that treats obesity as a chronic disease — not a personal failure — and integrates lifestyle changes with appropriate medical tools when needed. 

“This is not an either-or proposition.” 

“Diets fail because we’ve ignored biology. When we address it — through lifestyle, personalization, and appropriate medical therapy — people succeed (and for the long run).” 

#DrBourke #JumpstartMD #WeightLossScience #GLP1 #PersonalizedMedicine #MetabolicHealth #SustainableWeightLoss
